text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 63-year-old female with metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma, right kidney primary (status post cytoreductive right radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection and IVC thrombectomy). Known bilateral pulmonary metastases, right adrenal metastasis, and hepatic segment VI metastasis. Currently on systemic therapy with cabozantinib. Restaging evaluation to assess treatment response.

COMPARISON: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast from approximately 3 months prior (obtained at time of progression on nivolumab and transition to cabozantinib).

TECHNIQUE: Helical CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of 100 mL of iohexol (Omnipaque 350) intravenous contrast material. Images were acquired in the portal venous phase for the abdomen and pelvis and in a combined vascular/parenchymal phase for the chest. Axial images were reconstructed at 2.5 mm thickness throughout, with coronal and sagittal multiplanar reformats generated. Lung window, mediastinal window, and bone window reconstructions reviewed.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The central airways remain patent without endobronchial lesion. Moderate bilateral lung parenchymal heterogeneity is present, with background mild peribronchovascular thickening. The previously identified bilateral pulmonary metastases are again noted and are described individually:

- Right lower lobe: A partially treated lesion in the posterior basal segment of the right lower lobe, corresponding to the previously irradiated SBRT target, now measures approximately 1.4 cm in longest axis (previously 1.8 cm on the prior comparison scan). This lesion demonstrates peripheral ground-glass attenuation surrounding a residual soft tissue density core, a morphologic pattern consistent with post-radiation fibroinflammatory change superimposed on residual or treated tumor. There is no surrounding satellite nodularity or pleural involvement. Overall, this lesion appears mildly reduced relative to prior, in keeping with local treatment effect.

- Right upper lobe: A solid nodule in the posterior segment of the right upper lobe has decreased slightly in size, now measuring approximately 1.5 cm in longest axis (previously 1.8 cm). Margins remain well-defined and lobulated. No cavitation. No pleural tethering.

- Left lower lobe: A solid nodule in the posterobasal segment of the left lower lobe now measures approximately 1.2 cm (previously 1.4 cm). It maintains smooth to mildly irregular margins, consistent with prior characterization as a metastatic deposit.

- Left upper lobe: A subcentimeter nodule in the anterior segment of the left upper lobe now measures approximately 0.8 cm (previously 0.9 cm), mildly reduced from prior. This lesion remains well-circumscribed with no surrounding infiltrate.

No new pulmonary nodules or masses identified. No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. No evidence of pulmonary embolism on this non-dedicated examination.

Mediastinum and Hila: No significant mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. Cardiomediastinal silhouette is within normal limits. No pericardial effusion. Aorta and central pulmonary arteries appear unremarkable.

Chest Wall and Pleura: No pleural effusion. No pleural thickening. No rib lesions concerning for osseous metastatic disease. Post-thoracotomy changes are not present. Soft tissues of the chest wall appear unremarkable.

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size with smooth contours. A hypoenhancing lesion in hepatic segment VI, previously identified as a new metastatic deposit, now measures approximately 1.3 cm (previously 1.6 cm at the time of progression imaging). The lesion demonstrates mild heterogeneous internal enhancement on portal venous phase imaging, with a thin hypodense rim. This is mildly reduced in size compared to the prior examination, consistent with partial treatment effect. No satellite lesions. No biliary ductal dilatation. The portal and hepatic veins are patent.

No additional suspicious hepatic focal lesions are identified. No perihepatic ascites.

Gallbladder: The gallbladder is present and unremarkable. No cholelithiasis or cholecystitis.

Pancreas: The pancreas appears homogeneous without focal mass, ductal dilatation, or peripancreatic fat stranding.

Spleen: Normal in size and homogeneous attenuation without focal lesion.

Adrenal Glands: The left adrenal gland appears normal. The right adrenal gland demonstrates a nodular soft tissue focus measuring approximately 1.3 cm (previously 1.5 cm at the time of progression), mildly reduced in size. This is consistent with the previously biopsied metastatic right adrenal deposit from known ccRCC primary. Enhancement pattern on portal venous phase is nodular and heterogeneous. No surrounding infiltration or right adrenal vein involvement identified.

Kidneys and Retroperitoneum: Right nephrectomy bed is again identified with expected postsurgical fibrosis and soft tissue stranding in the right retroperitoneum. No discrete soft tissue mass or nodal recurrence in the right nephrectomy bed or right retroperitoneal chain. No new retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy identified. The left kidney is normal in size and morphology with normal corticomedullary differentiation and no hydronephrosis. No left renal mass. No renal calculi.

Aorta/Inferior Vena Cava: The aorta is normal in caliber. The IVC is patent without evidence of intraluminal thrombus, consistent with adequate postsurgical reconstruction. No new vascular abnormality.

Small and Large Bowel: Bowel loops are normal in caliber and wall thickness throughout. No obstruction, wall thickening, or mesenteric fat stranding to suggest inflammatory or neoplastic bowel involvement.

PERITONEUM AND PELVIS:

Peritoneum: No peritoneal nodularity or ascites to suggest peritoneal carcinomatosis.

Lymph Nodes: No pathologically enlarged pelvic or inguinal lymph nodes. The right iliac chain nodes previously identified at staging (right common iliac chain 1.5 cm) are no longer appreciable as distinct enlarged nodes, consistent with post-surgical and post-treatment normalization.

Bladder and Gynecologic Structures: The bladder is partially distended and unremarkable. The uterus and adnexa appear within normal limits for age.

Bone Windows: The visualized osseous structures are examined with bone window reconstruction. No focal lytic or sclerotic lesions to suggest osseous metastatic disease. Mild degenerative changes are present at the lumbar facet joints and in the lower thoracic spine, age-appropriate. No pathologic fracture.

Soft Tissues: Subcutaneous and deep soft tissues are unremarkable.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. BILATERAL PULMONARY METASTASES - MODESTLY DECREASED: All four bilateral pulmonary metastatic deposits demonstrate mild interval reduction in size compared to the prior examination. The right upper lobe lesion (approximately 1.5 cm, previously 1.8 cm), left lower lobe lesion (approximately 1.2 cm, previously 1.4 cm), and left upper lobe lesion (approximately 0.8 cm, previously 0.9 cm) have each mildly decreased. No new pulmonary nodules or masses.

2. RIGHT LOWER LOBE LESION - TREATED, LOCALLY STABLE TO MILDLY REDUCED: The previously irradiated right lower lobe target lesion (SBRT, 54 Gy in 3 fractions) now measures approximately 1.4 cm and demonstrates morphologic features of treatment effect with peripheral ground-glass change. This is marginally smaller than the prior scan measurement of 1.8 cm; the change is likely a combination of continued radiation response and possible cabozantinib effect.

3. HEPATIC SEGMENT VI METASTASIS - MILDLY DECREASED: The previously identified right hepatic lobe segment VI metastatic focus has mildly decreased in size to approximately 1.3 cm from 1.6 cm, consistent with a partial treatment response at this site.

4. RIGHT ADRENAL METASTASIS - MILDLY DECREASED: The biopsy-confirmed right adrenal metastasis has modestly decreased in size to approximately 1.3 cm from 1.5 cm, in keeping with mild treatment response.

5. NO NEW SITES OF METASTATIC DISEASE: No new pulmonary, hepatic, osseous, or soft tissue lesions identified. No intracranial imaging was performed on this study; clinical correlation regarding need for dedicated brain imaging is suggested.

6. STABLE POSTSURGICAL CHANGES: Right nephrectomy bed changes are stable. IVC is patent without thrombus. No retroperitoneal nodal recurrence.

Overall, findings are consistent with disease stabilization and mild treatment response across all measurable metastatic sites on the current cabozantinib regimen, with no evidence of new disease sites.

Interpreting Radiologist: [Attending Radiologist, Abdominal Imaging and Oncologic Imaging Section]
Dictated and Electronically Signed
